hero.title: Backend Systems in Production Conditions
hero.subtitle: Interactive platform exposing real metrics, controlled failures, and system behaviour under load.
hero.differentiator: This system intentionally injects failures to demonstrate resilience, degradation, and real-time recovery.
error.generic: An error occurred while loading data.
server.wakeup.loading: Waking up the server (free tier). Please wait a few seconds... 🚀
server.wakeup.error: The server took too long to respond. It may be starting up from a cold start. Please refresh the page in a few seconds.
contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.name: Name
contact.error: Error sending message. Please try again.
contact.placeholder.name: Your name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.placeholder.subject: Message subject
contact.error.email_invalid: Please enter a valid email address.
contact.error.subject_too_short: Subject must be at least 5 characters long.
contact.error.message_too_short: Message must be at least 10 characters long.
contact.error.duplicate: You already sent this message, please try again later or send a different message
contact.error.rate_limit: You have reached the limit of messages allowed for now. Please try again later.
metrics.section_subtitle: Metrics derived from real system traffic and execution. Not simulated or pre-computed.
metrics.source_tooltip: View raw JSON powering these tiles
chaos.title: Chaos Playground
chaos.subtitle: Break this system. Watch it recover.
chaos.disclaimer: Chaos scenarios are intentionally triggered in a controlled environment to demonstrate resilience patterns.
chaos.narrative: Designed to survive burst traffic without degrading latency. Rate-limited, idempotent, and observable by design.
philosophy.title: The starting point does not determine your destination
chaos.action.spike.title: Traffic Spike
chaos.action.spike.desc: Fires a burst of 30 concurrent requests. Observe latency spike in metrics above.
chaos.action.failure.title: Force Failure
chaos.action.failure.desc: Triggers a 503 condition. Watch system status shift to degraded.
chaos.action.cache.title: Cache Stress
chaos.action.cache.desc: 10 parallel reads. Client-side simulation — generates real backend load.
chaos.action.cache.disclaimer: Simulated via concurrent requests, not a backend-tracked incident.
chaos.note: Each action directly impacts the metrics visible above.
observability.title: Observability & Tracing
observability.subtitle: Every request can be inspected through structured logs and execution traces.
observability.empty: Trigger a chaos action to see traces appear here.
logs.title: System Logs
logs.subtitle: Structured event stream. Filter by level or correlate via request_id.
logs.empty: No log entries yet. Interact with the system to generate events.
about.title: Backend Engineering Focus
about.subtitle: Backend-oriented development focused on APIs and production operations.
about.bio: Interest in performance, resilience and observability. Building systems that fail gracefully, recover automatically, and expose everything through structured telemetry.
